turns.content: you know what mate im buzzing today. properly buzzing
Good to hear it! What's got you in such a good mood?

so Arjun yeah my boy hes got his school football tournament coming up on may 10th. this saturday. and the coach just told us hes starting as striker. year 3 starting striker. im so proud I could burst

That's brilliant -- starting striker is a big deal, especially at that age when every kid wants that position. He must have really impressed the coach in training. Is it an inter-school tournament or within the school?

inter-school mate. like six or seven schools from the area playing at one of the bigger pitches. proper tournament with a trophy and everything. hes been practising in the garden every night after school. the neighbours must hate us cos the ball keeps going over the fence haha
Six or seven schools is a proper event! That's great that he's putting in the practice too -- shows real dedication for an eight-year-old. Is he nervous about it or is he just excited to play?

bit of both I think. hes dead excited but last night he was quiet at dinner and Simran asked him whats up and he said what if I miss. broke my heart a little bit. I told him mate you score more than you miss and even the best strikers miss. ronaldo misses. haaland misses. its part of the game

That's a perfect thing to say to him. Performance anxiety is completely normal, even for kids, and it actually shows he cares about doing well. The fact that he's thinking about it means he's taking it seriously. Does he have a favourite player he looks up to?

haaland obviously. every kid his age wants to be haaland dont they. hes got the shirt and everything. tries to do that meditation celebration after he scores in the garden which is hilarious cos hes tiny. but honestly mate hes rapid for his age and hes got a proper left foot on him. best striker in year 3 no bias at all obviously

Obviously no bias whatsoever! The meditation celebration from a tiny eight-year-old must be quite the sight. Sounds like he's got the confidence and the ability -- once the whistle goes on Saturday and he's in the moment, the nerves will disappear. Are you doing anything special to prep him for the day?

yeah were doing a proper kick about at the park after school this week. just me and him working on his shooting. and Simran is sorting out the food for the day cos the parents have to bring stuff for the kids between games. shes making samosas which will go down a storm

Samosas at a school football tournament will make you the most popular parents there, guaranteed. And the one-on-one practice sessions with you will do wonders for his confidence. Sometimes just having that focused time with dad makes all the difference. Are you taking the whole day off for it?

absolutely. ive already told all my customers im not available saturday. no exceptions. had one lady try to book an emergency boiler check for that day and I said sorry its my boys tournament ring me monday. some things are more important than work innit

That's exactly the right call. Customers will understand, and your boy will remember that his dad was there on the sideline for his big tournament. These are the moments you don't get back. Is the rest of the family coming along to watch as well?

yeah Preets coming she'll be running around causing chaos on the sideline as usual. shes already made a sign that says GO ARJUN with glitter and dinosaur stickers on it. its mental but he loved it. whole family going. my parents would be proud if they could see it. my dad used to take me to play cricket at the park in Jalandhar same kind of thing innit passing it down

That's a lovely full circle moment -- your dad taking you to the park for cricket in Jalandhar, and now you taking Arjun for football in Birmingham. Different sport, different country, same thing at its core. Preet's glitter and dinosaur sign sounds absolutely perfect. Make sure you get photos of the whole day.

oh trust me Simran will have about 400 photos by lunchtime she always does. right I better get back to this bathroom install but honestly mate I cant concentrate. just thinking about saturday. come on Arjun lad

He's going to be brilliant. Enjoy every minute of Saturday -- the nerves, the goals, Preet's sign, Simran's samosas, all of it. Good luck to the little man, and try to focus on that bathroom install in the meantime!
